title: Cryptography
summary: The protocol vocabulary for understanding how confidential execution protects data while still producing usable outputs.

bodySections.title: Mathematical Foundation
bodySections.body: Cryptography content translates the protocol layer into product consequences. It frames how Multi-Party Computation, verification, and encrypted coordination let Arcium expose useful application behavior without exposing the inputs that produced it.

bodySections.title: Why readers should care
bodySections.body: This category exists to keep the math attached to real questions. The goal is not to turn every reader into a cryptographer, but to make the security story legible enough that product claims can be judged more rigorously.

bodySections.title: What belongs here
bodySections.body: Use this category for concepts like proof systems, confidentiality guarantees, trust assumptions, and the language needed to separate a genuine privacy model from vague marketing shorthand.
